[[:Media:{{{1}}}|{{{2}}}]] ([[Wikipedia:Media help|help]]·[[:Image:{{{1}}}|info]]) __NOTOC__ ==Purpose== This template links to an audio file for quick listening. It is meant to be used inline with text, for pronunciations and the like. ==Usage== '''{{Audio|name of sound file|text to use as link to soundfile}}''' Example: :'''{{Audio|Fr-Bordeaux.ogg|pronunciation}}''' gives this: :'''Bordeaux''' ({{audio|Fr-Bordeaux.ogg|pronunciation}}) is a [[Seaport|port]] city in... ==See Also== *{{tl|Audio-nohelp}}, a variant of this template with no help link. *{{tl|Listen}} [[de:Vorlage:Audio]] [[pl:Szablon:Audio]] [[th:Template:เสียง]] [[vi:Tiêu bản:Âm thanh]]

[[:Media:{{{1}}}|{{{2}}}]] ([[Wikipedia:Media help|help]]·[[:Image:{{{1}}}|info]]) __NOTOC__ ==Purpose== This template links to an audio file for quick listening. It is meant to be used inline with text, for pronunciations and the like. == Usage == {{Audio|name of sound file|text to use as link to soundfile}} Example: :'''Bordeaux''' ({{Audio|Fr-Bordeaux.ogg|pronunciation}}) is a [[Seaport|port]] city in... gives this: :'''Bordeaux''' ({{Audio|Fr-Bordeaux.ogg|pronunciation}}) is a [[Seaport|port]] city in... == See also == * {{tl|Audio-nohelp}}, a variant of this template with no help links. * {{tl|Audio-IPA}}, a variant of this template to be used with [[International Phonetic Alphabet|IPA]] notation. **{{tl|Audio-IPA-nohelp}}, a variant with no help links. * {{tl|Spoken}}, which adds only the small icon with no links. * {{tl|Listen}}, which sets an audio clip off from the main text. revision.comment: i get asked about this a lot
revision.text: [[:Media:{{{1|}}}|{{{2|{{{1|}}}}}}]] ([[Wikipedia:Media help|help]]·[[:Image:{{{1|}}}|info]]) __NOTOC__ {{protected template}} == Purpose == This template links to an audio file for quick listening. It is meant to be used inline with text, for pronunciations and the like. == Usage == {{Audio|name of sound file|text to use as link to soundfile}} Example: :'''Bordeaux''' ({{Audio|Fr-Bordeaux.ogg|pronunciation}}) is a [[Seaport|port]] city in... gives this: :'''Bordeaux''' ({{Audio|Fr-Bordeaux.ogg|pronunciation}}) is a [[Seaport|port]] city in... == Using on other wikis == This is one of several templates that use [[CSS]] to format the template instead of containing all the formatting inline. '''It will not work on another wiki unless you copy both the template and the CSS rules.''' This particular template depends on the "audiolink" css class to add the loudspeaker icon as a background image for the link, which allows it to be clicked and go to the same link destination instead of the image's description page. These CSS rules are in [[MediaWiki:Common.css]] (the part that starts with .audiolink a {). You will need to get an administrator on your local wiki to add the rules to your site's CSS. (Likewise, tweaking the size and placement of the icon is done by changing the CSS; not the template itself.) == See also == * {{tl|Audio-nohelp}}, a variant of this template with no help links. * {{tl|Audio-IPA}}, a variant of this template to be used with [[International Phonetic Alphabet|IPA]] notation. **{{tl|Audio-IPA-nohelp}}, a variant with no help links. * {{tl|Spoken}}, which adds only the small icon with no links. * {{tl|Listen}}, which sets an audio clip off from the main text. revision.comment: Use margin-right instead of nbsp between speaker icon and label so that text aggregators see it as "(audio)" instead of "( audio)". This affects link preview, Google, DuckDuckGo, Apple Look Up etc.

[[:Media:{{{1}}}|{{{2}}}]] ([[Wikipedia:Media help|help]]·[[:Image:{{{1}}}|info]]) __NOTOC__ ==Purpose== This template links to an audio file for quick listening. It is meant to be used inline with text, for pronunciations and the like. ==Usage== '''{{Audio|name of sound file|text to use as link to soundfile}}''' Example: :'''{{Audio|Fr-Bordeaux.ogg|pronunciation}}''' gives this: :'''Bordeaux''' ({{audio|Fr-Bordeaux.ogg|pronunciation}}) is a [[Seaport|port]] city in... ==See Also== *{{tl|Audio-nohelp}}, a variant of this template with no help link. *{{tl|Listen}} [[de:Vorlage:Audio]] [[pl:Szablon:Audio]] [[th:Template:เสียง]] [[vi:Tiêu bản:Âm thanh]]

It is meant to be used inline with text, for pronunciations and the like. == Usage == {{Audio|name of sound file|text to use as link to soundfile}} Example: :{{Audio|Fr-Bordeaux.ogg|pronunciation}} gives this: : '''Bordeaux''' ({{audio|Fr-Bordeaux.ogg|pronunciation}}) is a [[Seaport|port]] city in... == See also == * {{tl|Audio-nohelp}}, a variant of this template with no help link. * {{tl|Spoken}}, which adds only the small icon with no links. * {{tl|Listen}}, which sets an audio clip off from the main text. [[de:Vorlage:Audio]] [[es:Plantilla:Audio]] [[fa:الگو:صدا]] [[pl:Szablon:Audio]] [[pt:Predefinição:Áudio]] [[th:Template:เสียง]] [[vi:Tiêu bản:Âm thanh]]

revision.text: [[:Media:{{{1|}}}|{{{2|{{{1|}}}}}}]] ([[Wikipedia:Media help|help]]·[[:Image:{{{1|}}}|info]]) __NOTOC__ {{protected template}} == Purpose == This template links to an audio file for quick listening. It is meant to be used inline with text, for pronunciations and the like. == Usage == {{Audio|name of sound file|text to use as link to soundfile}} Example: :'''Bordeaux''' ({{Audio|Fr-Bordeaux.ogg|pronunciation}}) is a [[Seaport|port]] city in... gives this: :'''Bordeaux''' ({{Audio|Fr-Bordeaux.ogg|pronunciation}}) is a [[Seaport|port]] city in... == Using on other wikis == This is one of several templates that use [[CSS]] to format the template instead of containing all the formatting inline. '''It will not work on another wiki unless you copy both the template and the CSS rules.''' This particular template depends on the "audiolink" css class to add the loudspeaker icon as a background image for the link, which allows it to be clicked and go to the same link destination instead of the image's description page. These CSS rules are in [[MediaWiki:Common.css]] (the part that starts with .audiolink a {). You will need to get an administrator on your local wiki to add the rules to your site's CSS. (Likewise, tweaking the size and placement of the icon is done by changing the CSS; not the template itself.) == See also == * {{tl|Audio-nohelp}}, a variant of this template with no help links. * {{tl|Audio-IPA}}, a variant of this template to be used with [[International Phonetic Alphabet|IPA]] notation. **{{tl|Audio-IPA-nohelp}}, a variant with no help links. * {{tl|Spoken}}, which adds only the small icon with no links. * {{tl|Listen}}, which sets an audio clip off from the main text. [[:Media:{{{1}}}|{{{2}}}]] ([[Wikipedia:Media help|help]]·[[:Image:{{{1}}}|info]]) __NOTOC__ ==Purpose== This template links to an audio file for quick listening. It is meant to be used inline with text, for pronunciations and the like. == Usage == {{Audio|name of sound file|text to use as link to soundfile}} Example: :'''Bordeaux''' ({{Audio|Fr-Bordeaux.ogg|pronunciation}}) is a [[Seaport|port]] city in... gives this: :'''Bordeaux''' ({{Audio|Fr-Bordeaux.ogg|pronunciation}}) is a [[Seaport|port]] city in... == See also == * {{tl|Audio-nohelp}}, a variant of this template with no help links. * {{tl|Audio-IPA}}, a variant of this template to be used with [[International Phonetic Alphabet|IPA]] notation. **{{tl|Audio-IPA-nohelp}}, a variant with no help links. * {{tl|Spoken}}, which adds only the small icon with no links. * {{tl|Listen}}, which sets an audio clip off from the main text. revision.comment: i get asked about this a lot
revision.text: [[:Media:{{{1|}}}|{{{2|{{{1|}}}}}}]] ([[Wikipedia:Media help|help]]·[[:Image:{{{1|}}}|info]]) __NOTOC__ {{protected template}} == Purpose == This template links to an audio file for quick listening. It is meant to be used inline with text, for pronunciations and the like. == Usage == {{Audio|name of sound file|text to use as link to soundfile}} Example: :'''Bordeaux''' ({{Audio|Fr-Bordeaux.ogg|pronunciation}}) is a [[Seaport|port]] city in... gives this: :'''Bordeaux''' ({{Audio|Fr-Bordeaux.ogg|pronunciation}}) is a [[Seaport|port]] city in... == Using on other wikis == This is one of several templates that use [[CSS]] to format the template instead of containing all the formatting inline. '''It will not work on another wiki unless you copy both the template and the CSS rules.''' This particular template depends on the "audiolink" css class to add the loudspeaker icon as a background image for the link, which allows it to be clicked and go to the same link destination instead of the image's description page. These CSS rules are in [[MediaWiki:Common.css]] (the part that starts with .audiolink a {). You will need to get an administrator on your local wiki to add the rules to your site's CSS. (Likewise, tweaking the size and placement of the icon is done by changing the CSS; not the template itself.) == See also == * {{tl|Audio-nohelp}}, a variant of this template with no help links. * {{tl|Audio-IPA}}, a variant of this template to be used with [[International Phonetic Alphabet|IPA]] notation. **{{tl|Audio-IPA-nohelp}}, a variant with no help links. * {{tl|Spoken}}, which adds only the small icon with no links. * {{tl|Listen}}, which sets an audio clip off from the main text. revision.comment: Use margin-right instead of nbsp between speaker icon and label so that text aggregators see it as "(audio)" instead of "( audio)". This affects link preview, Google, DuckDuckGo, Apple Look Up etc.
